must_have_breakdown.skill: System Requirements writing and System Architecture Design
RADAR System (End User function) and Product knowledge
Experience on system service function (Diagnostics, Flashability, FOTA, etc) development
Basic Functional Safety and Cyber Security knowledge
Experience in RADAR function validation (HIL and vehicle environment)
Requirement management tools DOORs / Code Beamer and Configuration management tools such as CoreALM

reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Salesforce DevOps Engineer with zero relevance to the System Radar Function Owner role. Not a single must-have skill is met: no RADAR systems knowledge, no ASPICE or ISO26262 exposure, no embedded systems or automotive protocol experience (CAN/LIN/Ethernet/LVDS), no system requirements writing or architecture design, and no requirement management tools like DOORs or Code Beamer. The candidate's entire career has been in Salesforce CI/CD pipelines and release engineering — a completely different industry and technology stack. There is no ambiguity here and no pool-thin exception applies. Do not proceed with this profile.
strengths: SALESFORCE DEVOPS DEPTH: Five years of hands-on ownership across Jenkins, GitHub Actions, Copado, Gearset, and Flosum — but entirely irrelevant to this automotive radar systems role.

CI/CD PIPELINE DELIVERY: Quantified deployment time reduction of 30-40% through incremental deployment strategy at Capgemini — impressive in its own domain but zero transferability to embedded radar system engineering.

CERTIFICATIONS PORTFOLIO: Holds seven Salesforce and DevOps-specific certifications including Copado Certified Consultant and multiple Salesforce credentials — again, none applicable to automotive systems engineering.
gaps: ZERO AUTOMOTIVE / RADAR DOMAIN EXPERIENCE: The candidate has no exposure to RADAR systems, embedded systems, automotive communication protocols (CAN/LIN/Ethernet/LVDS), or vehicle-level validation environments — these are the core competencies the role demands.

NO FUNCTIONAL SAFETY OR STANDARDS KNOWLEDGE: ASPICE, ISO26262, Functional Safety, and Cyber Security are all mandatory for this role and are completely absent from the candidate's background, making this a hard disqualification.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated; current role is active at Capgemini but irrelevant given domain mismatch.
Candidate is based in Chennai, matching the JD's in-office location requirement — no penalty.
Candidate has 5 years total experience vs 4 years relevant required, so no experience-volume penalty, though domain is entirely wrong.
JD gender preference is 'Any' — no penalty applicable.
No college preference or exclusion specified in the JD; SRM Institute is not flagged.

hard_filter_failures: Wrong domain — candidate has zero relevant experience in automotive radar systems engineering
availability_reason: Current role at Capgemini shows 'May 2025 – Present' with no end date or notice period stated.
alternative_role_fit: Senior Salesforce DevOps Engineer / Salesforce Release Engineer
rare_assets: Flosum Certified Professional — a niche certification rarely seen even among senior Salesforce DevOps practitioners.
Multi-tool Salesforce DevOps expertise spanning Copado, Gearset, Flosum, and GitHub Actions simultaneously — uncommon breadth within the Salesforce ecosystem.

Three achievement awards (Best Performer, Special Achievement, Learning Achievement) listed without context on scope, team size, or adjudicating body.
interview_flags: Clarify why this candidate is applying for an automotive radar systems engineering role given zero domain overlap — potential application error or role misunderstanding.
Probe whether candidate has any undisclosed exposure to embedded systems or automotive projects not reflected in the resume.
Ask about the nature of 'security scans' using Checkmarx — verify if any functional safety awareness exists even tangentially.
Verify the actual scale of Salesforce orgs managed at TCS — headcount, transaction volume, and org complexity are missing from the resume.
Confirm employment continuity — TCS end date (May 2025) overlaps with Capgemini start date (May 2025), which should be clarified.
